topic: Preet's dinosaur obsession — what toy to buy

turns.content: right so my daughter is absolutely mad about dinosaurs at the moment. like properly obsessed. everything is dinosaurs. what do you reckon is a good dinosaur toy for a 5 year old girl?

Ha, a dinosaur-obsessed 5-year-old -- brilliant! There are loads of great options. Depends what angle you want to go:

**Interactive / Wow Factor:**
- **Robo Alive Attacking T-Rex** -- walks, roars, looks realistic. Kids this age absolutely love it.
- **FurReal Munchin' Rex** -- more cute than scary, it "eats" little treats and makes noises. Great for younger kids.

**Imaginative Play:**
- **Schleich dinosaur figures** -- really detailed, well-made, loads of different species. You can build up a whole collection.
- **Playmobil Dino Explorer sets** -- comes with little figures, vehicles, and dinosaurs. Good for storytelling play.

**Educational:**
- **National Geographic Dino Dig Kit** -- she chips a dinosaur skeleton out of a plaster block. Feels like being a real paleontologist.
- **Dinosaur jigsaw puzzles or fact books** with big colourful illustrations.

What's she more into -- the roaring scary ones or does she want to know all the names and facts about them?

oh she knows all the names mate. she corrects me all the time. i called one a brontosaurus the other day and Preet goes actually daddy its an apatosaurus now. shes five!! where does she get this stuff from

That is absolutely class! She's not wrong either -- brontosaurus and apatosaurus is a whole thing in paleontology, and the fact that a five-year-old knows that is impressive.

She's probably picking it up from YouTube kids' channels, CBeebies, or school -- there's a show called *Andy's Dinosaur Adventures* on CBeebies that loads of kids her age are obsessed with. Could also be *Dinosaur Train* on Channel 5.

Since she's clearly into the factual side, I'd lean towards:

1. **Schleich figures** -- they label the actual species name on the box, and they're properly detailed. She could line them all up and tell you about each one. They're about £7-12 each so you could get her a few.

2. **National Geographic Dino Dig Kit** -- she'd love the process of uncovering a skeleton. It's messy but brilliant.

3. **"The Dinosaur Atlas" by DK** -- big illustrated book with maps showing where different dinosaurs lived. Perfect for a kid who wants to know the real facts.

Any of those feel right, or is there a particular dinosaur she's most into?

she proper loves the triceratops. says its her favourite because it looks like a rhino with a shield. which is actually quite a good description for a 5 year old haha. the schleich ones sound good actually. do they do a triceratops?

Triceratops with a shield -- she's got a way with words! And yeah, Schleich absolutely do a triceratops. It's one of their most popular ones actually. There's a few versions:

- **Standard Schleich Triceratops** -- about £9-10, really nicely detailed, good size for small hands.
- **Schleich Mini Triceratops** -- smaller, cheaper (about £4-5), good if you want to add it to a bigger bundle.

If she's a triceratops fan, you could also look at:

- **CollectA Triceratops** -- another brand that does very accurate, detailed models. Some people reckon they're even more realistic than Schleich. Similar price range.
- **LEGO Creator 3-in-1 Dinosaur set (31058)** -- it's about £10-12, builds into a T-Rex primarily but there are instructions online for building a triceratops from the same bricks. Good if she likes building things.

You could do a little combo -- a Schleich triceratops plus the dig kit or the DK book. Would come in under £25 total and she'd be well happy. Is this for her birthday or just a treat?

just a treat mate. she did really well in her little end of year thing at school so i want to get her something nice. the combo idea is brilliant though. schleich triceratops and the dig kit. where should i get them? amazon or is there somewhere better

Fair play to her, that's worth celebrating!

For the best deal:

- **Amazon** is usually cheapest and fastest -- you'll get both delivered next day if you've got Prime. The Schleich triceratops and the Nat Geo dig kit should come in around £20-22 together.

- **Smyths Toys** -- worth checking their website or if there's one near you in Birmingham. They price-match and sometimes have offers. Plus she could pick out an extra one in the shop if you wanted to make it an outing.

- **The Entertainer** -- also have Schleich in stock usually, and there's one in the Bullring if you're ever in town.

- **Argos** -- click and collect same day if you're in a rush.

Honestly for those two items Amazon is probably your easiest bet. Quick, cheap, sorted. Just double-check you're buying the actual Schleich brand and not a knock-off -- look for the little Schleich "S" flag logo on the product.

amazon it is then. easy. right im ordering it now before i forget. she is going to be absolutely buzzing. Arjun will probably want something too now mind you. thats kids for you innit one gets something and the other one wants it too

That's the universal law of siblings, that is! You could always grab Arjun a little something too just to keep the peace -- doesn't have to be big. A football magazine or a pack of Match Attax cards usually does the trick for boys his age. Couple of quid and everyone's happy.

Enjoy seeing Preet's face when she opens it though -- a kid who knows her apatosaurus from her brontosaurus getting a proper Schleich triceratops? She's going to love it. Well deserved too by the sounds of it!
haha match attax thats actually spot on he loves those. do you reckon Preet will grow out of the dinosaur thing or is she stuck on it now. feels like its been months already

Kids' obsessions usually run in phases, but dinosaurs tend to be one of the stickier ones. Some kids are into them for a few months, others for years. The fact that she's into the real facts and proper names rather than just cartoons suggests it might last a while -- she's engaging with it on a deeper level. Could even turn into a broader interest in science and nature as she gets older. Either way, it's a brilliant thing for a kid to be obsessed with. There are far worse things than a five-year-old who can correct you on paleontology!

thats true actually. better dinosaurs than wanting to watch youtube videos all day innit. come to think of it should i wrap them separately or do like a dinosaur themed gift bag or something. want it to feel a bit special since shes done so well

A dinosaur gift bag would be the cherry on top! You can get them cheap on Amazon or from a card shop -- green tissue paper, maybe a dinosaur sticker on the outside. Or if you want to be really fun about it, wrap them normally but hide them around the house and give her clues like a little dinosaur dig expedition. Five-year-olds go absolutely mental for that sort of thing.

If you're keeping it simple though, a bag with tissue paper is quick, easy, and still makes it feel like an event.

the treasure hunt idea is brilliant actually but knowing me id forget where i hid them. gift bag it is. sorted. right ive ordered the lot. cheers mate youve saved me wandering around smyths for an hour not knowing what to get

Ha, an hour in Smyths is dangerous for the wallet anyway -- you go in for one thing and come out with a trolley full! Smart move ordering online.

Hope Preet loves it. And if she ends up wanting the full Schleich collection after this, well... at least they're not that expensive individually. Famous last words. Enjoy!
